notes.note: M. Terentius Varro of Reate, the polymath and a close associate of Pompey. Here he serves as the channel reporting Pompey's intentions toward Cicero's recall.

notes.anchor: Pompey would certainly take up our cause
notes.note: Pompey's backing was the pivot of Cicero's hopes for recall, but in autumn 58 he held off pending Caesar's assent. His eventual support in 57 proved decisive.

notes.anchor: the letter he was waiting for from Caesar
notes.note: Pompey would not move on Cicero's recall without Caesar's agreement; Caesar was campaigning in Gaul and his correspondence governed the timing. Cicero's fate hinged on the alignment of the two dynasts.

notes.anchor: appoint a man to manage the case
notes.note: An actor: an agent or proposer who would formally push the recall bill through the magistrates and assemblies.
The consular and other elections for 57 BC, after which the incoming, friendlier magistrates could take up Cicero's cause.
